title: WISP Networking Protocol Flow

fields: streamMap: Map<streamID, {queue, streamID, buffer, dataCallBack, closeCallBack}>

steps: Call setup() → create new WebSocket(wispURL), set binaryType='arraybuffer'
Register ws.onmessage handler
On incoming packet: parse with parseIncomingPacket(Uint8Array(event.data))
Switch on packetType:
[DATA] → lookup stream in streamMap, call stream.dataCallBack(payload.slice(0))
[CONTINUE, streamID=0] → set this._bufferMax = remainingBuffer; register ws.onclose → setTimeout(setup, 1000); call this.onReady() if set
[CONTINUE, streamID>0] → update stream.buffer = remainingBuffer; call this._continue(streamID)
[CLOSE, streamID!=0] → call stream.closeCallBack(reason)
[INFO] → if puterAuth, send INFO packet with streamID=0 and puterAuth token
Allocate streamID = this._nextStreamID++
Store in streamMap: { queue:[], streamID, buffer: _bufferMax, dataCallBack, closeCallBack }
Send CONNECT packet via ws.send(createWispPacket({ CONNECT, TCP, streamID, hostname: host, port }))
Return streamID to caller
Lookup streamData from streamMap
[DECISION] streamData.buffer > 0?
YES → decrement buffer; send DATA packet via ws.send(createWispPacket({ DATA, streamID, payload: data }))
NO  → push data onto streamData.queue (backpressure)
Get stream's queue from streamMap
Loop over queued payloads: call this.write(streamID, queue.shift()) for each
Drains buffered writes now that buffer space has been replenished (triggered by CONTINUE packet)
Send CLOSE packet via ws.send(createWispPacket({ CLOSE, streamID, reason: 0x02 }))
No local cleanup of streamMap entry (stream entry persists until server sends CLOSE back)

key_conditions: CONTINUE with streamID=0 → initial handshake, sets global _bufferMax, fires onReady callback
CONTINUE with streamID>0 → per-stream flow control, drains queue via _continue()
write() buffer==0 → data queued instead of sent (backpressure)
WebSocket close → reconnect after 1000ms via setup()
dependencies: parsers.js: parseIncomingPacket, createWispPacket, CLOSE, CONNECT, DATA, CONTINUE, INFO, TCP, UDP, textde
WispRelayService (backend): spawns relay process with PORT and WISP_AUTH_SERVER env vars
WispService (backend): separate backend service for WISP
important_notes: PWispHandler is the client-side (puter-js) WISP protocol driver over a WebSocket. It manages multiple multiplexed streams via streamMap, each with its own buffer counter for flow control. The CONTINUE packet (streamID=0) is the initial ready signal; per-stream CONTINUE replenishes buffer and drains queued payloads. INFO packet triggers authentication by sending puterAuth token back to server. Reconnect logic uses setTimeout(setup, 1000) on ws close.
